agent_output: {"executive_summary":"A logistics API experiencing gradual P99 latency increase from 200ms to 1800ms over two weeks, with no deployment correlation, normal DB query times, and no errors. The symptom profile — gradual onset, success with slowness, external API dependencies, and normal DB metrics — strongly points to degradation in one or more external API dependencies (geocoding, carrier rates, or payment gateway), compounded by likely sequential calling of those APIs without proper timeout/circuit-breaker protections. Secondary hypotheses include connection pool exhaustion, memory/GC pressure from a slow leak, and DNS resolution degradation.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Logistics API Service","responsibility":"Orchestrates shipment quoting, booking, and payment by coordinating external services and internal data","boundaries":"Owns request lifecycle, timeout policies, retry logic, and response composition"},{"name":"Geocoding Service (External)","responsibility":"Resolves addresses to coordinates for distance/zone calculations","boundaries":"Third-party dependency, latency outside direct control"},{"name":"Carrier Rates API (External)","responsibility":"Provides shipping rate quotes from carriers","boundaries":"Third-party dependency, may have rate limiting or degraded performance windows"},{"name":"Payment Gateway (External)","responsibility":"Processes payment authorization and capture","boundaries":"Third-party dependency, PCI-scoped integration"},{"name":"Database","responsibility":"Stores shipments, orders, customer data","boundaries":"Already ruled out as primary cause — dashboard shows normal query times"}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Logistics API Service","to":"Geocoding Service","contract":"HTTP REST — address in, coordinates out","coupling_type":"temporal (synchronous call in request path)"},{"from":"Logistics API Service","to":"Carrier Rates API","contract":"HTTP REST — origin/destination/weight in, rates out","coupling_type":"temporal (synchronous call in request path)"},{"from":"Logistics API Service","to":"Payment Gateway","contract":"HTTP REST — payment details in, authorization out","coupling_type":"temporal (synchronous call in request path)"},{"from":"Logistics API Service","to":"Database","contract":"SQL/ORM queries","coupling_type":"temporal (synchronous, but measured normal)"}],"data_flow":"Request arrives → DB lookup for context → Geocoding call → Carrier Rates call → Payment Gateway call → Response composed and returned. If these external calls are sequential, total latency = sum of all three.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Hypothesis-Driven Diagnosis","rationale":"Multiple plausible root causes exist; systematic elimination is required rather than guessing"},{"pattern":"Circuit Breaker (recommended, likely missing)","rationale":"Absence of circuit breakers allows a degrading external service to drag down the entire API"},{"pattern":"Bulkhead (recommended, likely missing)","rationale":"Without isolation, one slow external dependency can exhaust the connection/thread pool and affect all requests"}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Shipment Orchestration","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment","Quote","Route"]},{"name":"External Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["GeocodingAdapter","CarrierRatesAdapter","PaymentAdapter"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Geocoding Service","downstream":"Shipment Orchestration","relationship":"Conformist — the logistics service conforms to the external geocoding model"},{"upstream":"Carrier Rates API","downstream":"Shipment Orchestration","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer (recommended) — carrier rate models should be translated to internal domain"},{"upstream":"Payment Gateway","downstream":"Shipment Orchestration","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer — payment concepts translated to internal payment model"}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Diagnose external API degradation as primary hypothesis over internal causes","gains":"Matches all symptom characteristics: gradual onset, no errors, normal DB, success-but-slow","sacrifices":"Could miss a subtle internal cause (e.g., memory leak) that coincidentally matches the pattern","alternatives_considered":["Connection pool exhaustion","Memory leak / GC pressure","DNS resolution degradation","Thread starvation","Upstream load balancer misconfiguration"]},{"decision":"Recommend adding per-dependency observability before architectural changes","gains":"Evidence-based remediation; avoids solving the wrong problem","sacrifices":"Delays the fix by requiring instrumentation first","alternatives_considered":["Immediately add timeouts and circuit breakers without diagnosis","Blindly add caching to all external calls"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"External API integration layer","evolution_path":"Introduce adapter pattern with per-service timeouts, circuit breakers, and fallback strategies. Can later evolve to async/event-driven integration if latency requirements tighten."},{"area":"Caching for geocoding results","evolution_path":"Geocoding results are highly cacheable (addresses rarely change). A simple LRU or Redis cache could eliminate one external call entirely for repeated addresses."},{"area":"Parallel external API calls","evolution_path":"If geocoding and carrier rates are independent, execute them concurrently to reduce total wall-clock time from sum to max of the two."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Payment gateway must remain synchronous in the request path","reason":"Payment authorization is typically required before confirming a booking — cannot be deferred or cached safely"},{"area":"External API contracts","reason":"No control over third-party API performance characteristics, rate limits, or deprecation timelines"}],"risks":[{"risk":"One or more external APIs have degraded and will continue to worsen, eventually causing timeouts or cascading failures","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Immediately instrument per-dependency latency. Add aggressive timeouts (e.g., 2s per external call) and circuit breakers to prevent cascade."},{"risk":"Sequential external API calls mean latency is additive — even moderate degradation in each compounds to severe P99 impact","severity":"high","mitigation":"Identify call dependencies and parallelize where possible. Even if only geocoding + carrier rates can be parallel, that halves the external call contribution."},{"risk":"Absence of per-dependency latency metrics means the team is flying blind — they only see aggregate latency and DB latency","severity":"high","mitigation":"Add structured logging or distributed tracing (e.g., OpenTelemetry) with spans per external call. This is the single highest-priority action."},{"risk":"Connection pool or HTTP client exhaustion under slow responses — slow external calls hold connections longer, reducing available pool for new requests","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Audit HTTP client pool settings. Ensure max connections and idle timeouts are configured. Consider separate HTTP client pools per external service (bulkhead pattern)."},{"risk":"Memory leak or GC pressure from accumulated in-flight requests during slow periods","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Check memory and GC metrics over the same two-week window. If heap usage is climbing, investigate object retention."},{"risk":"DNS TTL or resolution changes for external service endpoints causing lookup delays","severity":"low","mitigation":"Check DNS resolution times in the same timeframe. Consider DNS caching at the application or infrastructure level."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase 1: OBSERVE — Add per-dependency latency instrumentation immediately. Tag each external call with service name and measure duration independently. Deploy and collect 24h of data. This will pinpoint which external service(s) degraded. Phase 2: PROTECT — Add timeouts (2s hard limit per external call), circuit breakers (trip after 5 failures in 30s window), and retry with backoff (1 retry, exponential). Phase 3: OPTIMIZE — Cache geocoding results (high cache-hit potential for repeated addresses). Parallelize independent external calls. Consider carrier rate caching with short TTL (30-60s) for identical route queries.","files_to_create_or_modify":["middleware/request_tracing.{ext} — per-request span tracking with external call breakdowns","adapters/geocoding_client.{ext} — add timeout, circuit breaker, caching layer","adapters/carrier_rates_client.{ext} — add timeout, circuit breaker, optional short-TTL cache","adapters/payment_client.{ext} — add timeout, circuit breaker","config/external_services.{ext} — centralized timeout/retry/circuit-breaker configuration","monitoring/dashboards — add per-external-service latency percentile panels"],"patterns_to_follow":"Adapter pattern for each external service. Circuit Breaker pattern (Hystrix-style or resilience4j/Polly equivalent). Bulkhead pattern with separate connection pools. Structured logging with correlation IDs. Cache-aside for geocoding.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT add retries without timeouts — retrying a slow call without a timeout makes things worse","Do NOT set timeouts too high — a 30s timeout on an external call defeats the purpose; 2-3s is appropriate for these services","Do NOT cache payment gateway responses — payment authorizations are not cacheable","Do NOT assume the DB dashboard is comprehensive — verify it includes connection acquisition time, not just query execution time","Do NOT add circuit breakers without fallback strategies — decide what happens when the circuit opens (return cached data? degrade gracefully? fail fast with clear error?)"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":78,"high_confidence_areas":["External API degradation is the primary hypothesis — the symptom profile is textbook for this failure mode","Per-dependency observability is missing and is the highest-priority remediation","Sequential external calls are compounding the latency impact"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which specific external API is degrading — cannot determine without per-dependency metrics","Whether connection pool exhaustion is a contributing secondary factor","Whether there is also a memory/GC component to the degradation"],"assumptions":["Database dashboard metrics are accurate and include connection acquisition time, not just query execution time","External API calls are made synchronously and sequentially in the request path","No significant traffic pattern change occurred in the same two-week window","The 10,000 requests/day volume has been relatively stable"]}}

content.text: {"executive_summary":"A logistics API experiencing gradual P99 latency increase from 200ms to 1800ms over two weeks, with no deployment correlation, normal DB query times, and no errors. The symptom profile — gradual onset, success with slowness, external API dependencies, and normal DB metrics — strongly points to degradation in one or more external API dependencies (geocoding, carrier rates, or payment gateway), compounded by likely sequential calling of those APIs without proper timeout/circuit-breaker protections. Secondary hypotheses include connection pool exhaustion, memory/GC pressure from a slow leak, and DNS resolution degradation.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Logistics API Service","responsibility":"Orchestrates shipment quoting, booking, and payment by coordinating external services and internal data","boundaries":"Owns request lifecycle, timeout policies, retry logic, and response composition"},{"name":"Geocoding Service (External)","responsibility":"Resolves addresses to coordinates for distance/zone calculations","boundaries":"Third-party dependency, latency outside direct control"},{"name":"Carrier Rates API (External)","responsibility":"Provides shipping rate quotes from carriers","boundaries":"Third-party dependency, may have rate limiting or degraded performance windows"},{"name":"Payment Gateway (External)","responsibility":"Processes payment authorization and capture","boundaries":"Third-party dependency, PCI-scoped integration"},{"name":"Database","responsibility":"Stores shipments, orders, customer data","boundaries":"Already ruled out as primary cause — dashboard shows normal query times"}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Logistics API Service","to":"Geocoding Service","contract":"HTTP REST — address in, coordinates out","coupling_type":"temporal (synchronous call in request path)"},{"from":"Logistics API Service","to":"Carrier Rates API","contract":"HTTP REST — origin/destination/weight in, rates out","coupling_type":"temporal (synchronous call in request path)"},{"from":"Logistics API Service","to":"Payment Gateway","contract":"HTTP REST — payment details in, authorization out","coupling_type":"temporal (synchronous call in request path)"},{"from":"Logistics API Service","to":"Database","contract":"SQL/ORM queries","coupling_type":"temporal (synchronous, but measured normal)"}],"data_flow":"Request arrives → DB lookup for context → Geocoding call → Carrier Rates call → Payment Gateway call → Response composed and returned. If these external calls are sequential, total latency = sum of all three.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Hypothesis-Driven Diagnosis","rationale":"Multiple plausible root causes exist; systematic elimination is required rather than guessing"},{"pattern":"Circuit Breaker (recommended, likely missing)","rationale":"Absence of circuit breakers allows a degrading external service to drag down the entire API"},{"pattern":"Bulkhead (recommended, likely missing)","rationale":"Without isolation, one slow external dependency can exhaust the connection/thread pool and affect all requests"}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Shipment Orchestration","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment","Quote","Route"]},{"name":"External Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["GeocodingAdapter","CarrierRatesAdapter","PaymentAdapter"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Geocoding Service","downstream":"Shipment calls"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"External API integration layer","evolution_path":"Introduce adapter pattern with per-service timeouts, circuit breakers, and fallback strategies. Can later evolve to async/event-driven integration if latency requirements tighten."},{"area":"Caching for geocoding results","evolution_path":"Geocoding results are highly cacheable (addresses rarely change). Even if only geocoding + carrier rates can be parallel, that halves the external call contribution."},{"risk":"Absence of per-dependency latency metrics means the team is flying blind — they only see aggregate latency and DB latency","severity":"high","mitigation":"Add structured logging or distributed tracing (e.g., OpenTelemetry) with spans per external call. Ensure max connections and idle timeouts are configured. Consider separate HTTP client pools per external service (bulkhead pattern)."},{"risk":"Memory leak or GC pressure from accumulated in-flight requests during slow periods","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Check memory and GC metrics over the same two-week window. Cache-aside for geocoding.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT add retries without timeouts — retrying a slow call without a timeout makes things worse","Do NOT set timeouts too high — a 30s timeout on an external call defeats the purpose; 2-3s is appropriate for these services","Do NOT cache payment gateway responses — payment authorizations are not cacheable","Do NOT assume the DB dashboard is comprehensive — verify it includes connection acquisition time, not just query execution time","Do NOT add circuit breakers without fallback strategies — decide what happens when the circuit opens (return cached data? degrade gracefully? fail fast with clear error?)"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":78,"high_confidence_areas":["External API degradation is the primary hypothesis — the symptom profile is textbook for this failure mode","Per-dependency observability is missing and is the highest-priority remediation","Sequential external calls are compounding the latency impact"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which specific external API is degrading — cannot determine without per-dependency metrics","Whether connection pool exhaustion is a contributing secondary factor","Whether there is also a memory/GC component to the degradation"],"assumptions":["Database dashboard metrics are accurate and include connection acquisition time, not just query execution time","External API calls are made synchronously and sequentially in the request path","No significant traffic pattern change occurred in the same two-week window","The 10,000 requests/day volume has been relatively stable"]}}
